sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES version

special_mechanics.name: Running
special_mechanics.description: Pressing joystick left or right twice quickly causes player to run. Running punch available via front attack button. Running automatically triggers flying kick on jump input. Back-kick available via rear attack button to halt and perform defensive move.

special_mechanics.name: Jump Kick
special_mechanics.description: While jumping, player can press either attack button for a jump kick towards that particular direction.

special_mechanics.name: Grab and Throw Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: When enemies are stunned after punches or kicks, player can approach for collar grab and kick repeatedly or perform shoulder throw. Boss characters require low health to be grabbed (except Sabu).

special_mechanics.name: Ground Stomp
special_mechanics.description: Pressing down over a fallen enemy allows player to sit on them and pummel with front attack button.

special_mechanics.name: Directional Movement
special_mechanics.description: 8-way directional movement (horizontal and vertical) in isometric arena space. Battle system limited to two directions despite 8-way movement capability.

special_mechanics.name: Combo System
special_mechanics.description: First punch temporarily immobilizes enemy, enabling successive punches without enemy defense. Enemies require succession of hits to defeat, more punishing than previous beat-em-ups.

tier_notes: Tier 2: Empirical/qualitative data only. Wikipedia source documents gameplay mechanics and control scheme but provides no quantitative physics parameters (speeds, acceleration, gravity values, frame counts). NES version documentation focuses on combat system design rather than movement physics.
